name: ENA binned metagenome
description: Minimum information to standardise metadata of binned metagenome samples. Ensures binned and MAG metagenome assembly metadata is compatible.

fields.label: relationship to oxygen
fields.name: relationship_to_oxygen
fields.helpText: Is this organism an aerobe, anaerobe? Please note that aerobic and anaerobic are valid descriptors for microbial environments

fields.label: sample collection method
fields.name: sample_collection_method
fields.helpText: The method employed for collecting the sample. Can be provided in the form of a PMID, DOI, url or text.
fields.group: sample collection: methods, storage and transport

fields.label: metagenomic source
fields.name: metagenomic_source
fields.helpText: The metagenomic source of the sample. This value should contain “metagenome” and be in the taxonomy database e.g. wastewater metagenome or human gut metagenome. Please note “metagenome” alone will not be accepted. Check here for more details on metagenome taxonomy: https://ena-docs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/faq_taxonomy.html#environmental-taxonomic-classifications

fields.label: sample derived from
fields.name: sample_derived_from
fields.helpText: Reference to parental sample(s) or original run(s) that the assembly is derived from. The referenced samples or runs should already be registered in INSDC. This should be formatted as one of the following. A single sample/run e.g. ERSxxxxxx OR a comma separated list e.g. ERSxxxxxx,ERSxxxxxx OR a range e.g. ERSxxxxxx-ERSxxxxxx

fields.label: project name
fields.name: project_name
fields.helpText: Name of the project within which the sequencing was organized

fields.label: multiplex identifiers
fields.name: multiplex_identifiers
fields.helpText: Molecular barcodes, called Multiplex Identifiers (MIDs), that are used to specifically tag unique samples in a sequencing run. Sequence should be reported in uppercase letters

fields.label: relevant electronic resources
fields.name: relevant_electronic_resources
fields.helpText: A related resource that is referenced, cited, or otherwise associated to the sequence in the format of a PMID, DOI or URL

fields.label: relevant standard operating procedures
fields.name: relevant_standard_operating_procedures
fields.helpText: Standard operating procedures used in assembly and/or annotation of genomes, metagenomes or environmental sequences in the format of a PMID, DOI or URL

fields.label: number of standard tRNAs extracted
fields.name: number_of_standard_trnas_extracted
fields.helpText: The total number of tRNAs identified from the bin, SAG or MAG

fields.label: 16s recovered
fields.name: 16s_recovered
fields.helpText: Can a 16S gene be recovered from the submitted bin, SAG or MAG?

fields.label: 16S recovery software
fields.name: 16s_recovery_software
fields.helpText: Tools used for 16S rRNA gene extraction. Add names and versions of software(s), parameters used

fields.label: tRNA extraction software
fields.name: trna_extraction_software
fields.helpText: Tools used for tRNA identification. Add names and versions of software(s), parameters used

fields.label: completeness score
fields.name: completeness_score
fields.helpText: Completeness score is typically based on either the fraction of markers found as compared to a database or the percent of a genome found as compared to a closely related reference genome. Completeness score is one of 3 attributes which in combination reflect the standard quality of a MAG, see here for more information: https://ena-docs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/faq_metagenomes.html. Mandatory for all samples directly linked with SAGs or MAGs.

fields.label: completeness software
fields.name: completeness_software
fields.helpText: Tools used for completion estimate, i.e. checkm, anvi'o, busco. Mandatory for all samples directly linked with SAGs or MAGs.

fields.label: completeness approach
fields.name: completeness_approach
fields.helpText: The approach used to determine the completeness of a given bin, SAG or MAG, which would typically make use of a set of conserved marker genes or a closely related reference genome. For UViG completeness, include reference genome or group used, and contig feature suggesting a complete genome

fields.label: contamination score
fields.name: contamination_score
fields.helpText: The contamination score is based on the fraction of single-copy genes that are observed more than once in a query genome. Contamination score is one of 3 attributes which in combination reflect the standard quality of a MAG, see here for more information: https://ena-docs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/faq_metagenomes.html. If contamination ≥ 10% then please submit as a binned assembly. In SAGs, contamination score is mandatory for prokaryotes and optional for eukaryotes.

fields.label: contamination screening input
fields.name: contamination_screening_input
fields.helpText: The type of sequence data used as input

fields.label: contamination screening parameters
fields.name: contamination_screening_parameters
fields.helpText: Specific parameters used in the decontamination sofware, such as reference database, coverage, and kmers. Combinations of these parameters may also be used, i.e. kmer and coverage, or reference database and kmer

fields.label: decontamination software
fields.name: decontamination_software
fields.helpText: Tool(s) used in contamination screening

fields.label: binning software
fields.name: binning_software
fields.helpText: Tool(s) used for the extraction of genomes from metagenomic datasets, where possible include a product ID (PID) of the tool(s) used. E.g. MetaCluster-TA (RRID:SCR_004599) or MaxBin (biotools:maxbin)

fields.label: reassembly post binning
fields.name: reassembly_post_binning
fields.helpText: Has an assembly been performed on a genome bin extracted from a metagenomic assembly?

fields.label: MAG coverage software
fields.name: mag_coverage_software
fields.helpText: Tool(s) used to determine the genome coverage if coverage is used as a binning parameter in the extraction of genomes from metagenomic datasets e.g. bwa, bbmap, bowtie, other

fields.label: assembly quality
fields.name: assembly_quality
fields.helpText: The assembly quality category is based on sets of criteria outlined for each assembly quality category. For MISAG/MIMAG; Finished: Single, validated, contiguous sequence per replicon without gaps or ambiguities with a consensus error rate equivalent to Q50 or better. High Quality Draft:Multiple fragments where gaps span repetitive regions. Presence of the 23S, 16S and 5S rRNA genes and at least 18 tRNAs. Medium Quality Draft:Many fragments with little to no review of assembly other than reporting of standard assembly statistics. Low Quality Draft:Many fragments with little to no review of assembly other than reporting of standard assembly statistics. Assembly statistics include, but are not limited to total assembly size, number of contigs, contig N50/L50, and maximum contig length. For MIUVIG; Finished: Single, validated, contiguous sequence per replicon without gaps or ambiguities, with extensive manual review and editing to annotate putative gene functions and transcriptional units. High-quality draft genome: One or multiple fragments, totaling ≥ 90% of the expected genome or replicon sequence or predicted complete. Genome fragment(s): One or multiple fragments, totalling < 90% of the expected genome or replicon sequence, or for which no genome size could be estimated.

options.value: Many fragments with little to no review of assembly other than reporting of standard assembly statistics
options.label: Many fragments with little to no review of assembly other than reporting of standard assembly statistics

options.value: Multiple fragments where gaps span repetitive regions. Presence of the 23S, 16S, and 5S rRNA genes and at least 18 tRNAs
options.label: Multiple fragments where gaps span repetitive regions. Presence of the 23S, 16S, and 5S rRNA genes and at least 18 tRNAs

options.value: Single contiguous sequence without gaps or ambiguities with a consensus error rate equivalent to Q50 or better
options.label: Single contiguous sequence without gaps or ambiguities with a consensus error rate equivalent to Q50 or better

fields.label: investigation type
fields.name: investigation_type
fields.helpText: Nucleic Acid Sequence Report is the root element of all MIxS compliant reports as standardised by Genomic Standards Consortium

fields.label: binning parameters
fields.name: binning_parameters
fields.helpText: The parameters that have been applied during the extraction of genomes from metagenomic datasets e.g. coverage and kmer

fields.label: taxonomic identity marker
fields.name: taxonomic_identity_marker
fields.helpText: The phylogenetic marker(s) used to assign an organism name to the bin, SAG or MAG. Examples are 16S gene, multi-marker approach or other e.g. rpoB gene. Mandatory for all samples directly linked with SAGs or MAGs.

fields.label: isolation_source
fields.name: isolation_source
fields.helpText: describes the physical, environmental and/or local geographical source of the biological sample from which the sample was derived

fields.label: collection date
fields.name: collection_date
fields.helpText: The date the sample was collected with the intention of sequencing, either as an instance (single point in time) or interval. In case no exact time is available, the date/time can be right truncated i.e. all of these are valid ISO8601 compliant times: 2008-01-23T19:23:10+00:00; 2008-01-23T19:23:10; 2008-01-23; 2008-01; 2008.

fields.label: altitude
fields.name: altitude
fields.helpText: The altitude of the sample is the vertical distance between Earth's surface above Sea Level and the sampled position in the air.

fields.label: geographic location (latitude)
fields.name: geographic_location_latitude
fields.helpText: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by latitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees and in WGS84 system

fields.label: geographic location (longitude)
fields.name: geographic_location_longitude
fields.helpText: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by longitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees and in WGS84 system

fields.label: broad-scale environmental context
fields.name: broad_scale_environmental_context
fields.helpText: Report the major environmental system the sample or specimen came from. The system(s) identified should have a coarse spatial grain, to provide the general environmental context of where the sampling was done (e.g. in the desert or a rainforest). We recommend using subclasses of EnvO’s biome class: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00000428. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS.

fields.label: local environmental context
fields.name: local_environmental_context
fields.helpText: Report the entity or entities which are in the sample or specimen’s local vicinity and which you believe have significant causal influences on your sample or specimen. We recommend using EnvO terms which are of smaller spatial grain than your entry for "broad-scale environmental context". Terms, such as anatomical sites, from other OBO Library ontologies which interoperate with EnvO (e.g. UBERON) are accepted in this field. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS.

fields.label: environmental medium
fields.name: environmental_medium
fields.helpText: Report the environmental material(s) immediately surrounding the sample or specimen at the time of sampling. We recommend using subclasses of 'environmental material' (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00010483). EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS . Terms from other OBO ontologies are permissible as long as they reference mass/volume nouns (e.g. air, water, blood) and not discrete, countable entities (e.g. a tree, a leaf, a table top).

fields.label: elevation
fields.name: elevation
fields.helpText: The elevation of the sampling site as measured by the vertical distance from mean sea level.

fields.label: amount or size of sample collected
fields.name: amount_or_size_of_sample_collected
fields.helpText: The total amount or size (volume (ml), mass (g) or area (m2) ) of sample collected.

fields.label: size fraction selected
fields.name: size_fraction_selected
fields.helpText: Filtering pore size used in sample preparation e.g. 0-0.22 micrometer

fields.label: geographic location (country and/or sea)
fields.name: geographic_location_country_and_or_sea
fields.helpText: The geographical origin of where the sample was collected from, with the intention of sequencing, as defined by the country or sea name. Country or sea names should be chosen from the INSDC country list (http://insdc.org/country.html).

options.value: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands
options.label: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands

fields.label: source material identifiers
fields.name: source_material_identifiers
fields.helpText: A unique identifier assigned to a material sample (as defined by http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/materialSampleID, and as opposed to a particular digital record of a material sample) used for extracting nucleic acids, and subsequent sequencing. The identifier can refer either to the original material collected or to any derived sub-samples. The INSDC qualifiers /specimen_voucher, /bio_material, or /culture_collection may or may not share the same value as the source_mat_id field. For instance, the /specimen_voucher qualifier and source_mat_id may both contain 'UAM:Herps:14' , referring to both the specimen voucher and sampled tissue with the same identifier. However, the /culture_collection qualifier may refer to a value from an initial culture (e.g. ATCC:11775) while source_mat_id would refer to an identifier from some derived culture from which the nucleic acids were extracted (e.g. xatc123 or ark:/2154/R2).

fields.label: experimental factor
fields.name: experimental_factor
fields.helpText: Experimental factors are essentially the variable aspects of an experiment design which can be used to describe an experiment, or set of experiments, in an increasingly detailed manner. This field accepts ontology terms from Experimental Factor Ontology (EFO) and/or Ontology for Biomedical Investigations (OBI). For a browser of EFO (v 2.95) terms, please see http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/EFO; for a browser of OBI (v 2018-02-12) terms please see http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/OBI. E.g. time series design [EFO:EFO_0001779]

fields.label: taxonomic classification
fields.name: taxonomic_classification
fields.helpText: Method used for taxonomic classification, along with reference database used, classification rank, and thresholds used to classify new genomes. Expected values are: classification method, database name, and other parameters e.g. vConTACT vContact2 (references from NCBI RefSeq v83, genus rank classification, default parameters)

fields.label: reference for biomaterial
fields.name: reference_for_biomaterial
fields.helpText: Primary publication if isolated before genome publication; otherwise, primary genome report. Mandatory for MIGS of bacteria and archaea.

fields.label: sample material processing
fields.name: sample_material_processing
fields.helpText: A brief description of any processing applied to the sample during or after retrieving the sample from environment, or a link to the relevant protocol(s) performed.

fields.label: nucleic acid extraction
fields.name: nucleic_acid_extraction
fields.helpText: A link to a literature reference, electronic resource or a standard operating procedure (SOP), that describes the material separation to recover the nucleic acid fraction from a sample

fields.label: nucleic acid amplification
fields.name: nucleic_acid_amplification
fields.helpText: A link to a literature reference, electronic resource or a standard operating procedure (SOP), that describes the enzymatic amplification (PCR, TMA, NASBA) of specific nucleic acids

fields.label: library size
fields.name: library_size
fields.helpText: Total number of clones in the library prepared for the project

fields.label: library reads sequenced
fields.name: library_reads_sequenced
fields.helpText: Total number of clones sequenced from the library

fields.label: library vector
fields.name: library_vector
fields.helpText: Cloning vector type(s) used in construction of libraries

fields.label: library screening strategy
fields.name: library_screening_strategy
fields.helpText: Specific enrichment or screening methods applied before and/or after creating clone libraries in order to select a specific group of sequences

fields.label: assembly software
fields.name: assembly_software
fields.helpText: Tool(s) used for assembly, including version number and parameters in the format {software};{version};{parameters} e.g. metaSPAdes;3.11.0;kmer set 21,33,55,77,99,121, default parameters otherwise

fields.label: adapters
fields.name: adapters
fields.helpText: Adapters provide priming sequences for both amplification and sequencing of the sample-library fragments. Both adapters should be reported; in uppercase letters

fields.label: depth
fields.name: depth
fields.helpText: The vertical distance below local surface, e.g. for sediment or soil samples depth is measured from sediment or soil surface, respectively. Depth can be reported as an interval for subsurface samples.
